creator: riggs@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to nhoyle from comment #2)

Yes, there is a good rationale.
The reason is that this is coming from _Rust's_ build system. It is _not_ something that originates in the ports framework.

Please also note that portmaster/portupgrade are not today's recommended tools for keeping a system up-to-date. pkg and poudriere are addressing most of their annoying shortcomings (most notably spurious linking problems that made up most of port build failures in the past) and should be used to keep custom ports up-to-date.
